summary: The VA STI carries forward the EJ257 engine with all its strengths and weaknesses. The same ringland and head gasket concerns persist, but the updated chassis and technology are improvements.

issues.name: Ringland Failure
issues.description: The EJ257 piston ringland cracking issue persists in the VA generation. The #4 cylinder runs hottest and is most susceptible, especially under high boost.

issues.name: Head Gasket Seepage
issues.description: The multi-layer steel head gaskets can develop external oil seepage or internal coolant/oil mixing. Monitor coolant levels and oil condition closely.

issues.name: Clutch Throwout Bearing
issues.description: The throwout bearing develops a chirping noise, especially in cooler weather. Replacement requires transmission removal.

issues.name: Intercooler Heat Soak
issues.description: The factory top-mount intercooler heat soaks rapidly during spirited driving, leading to power inconsistency and potentially dangerous knock on hot intake air.

issues.name: Infotainment Lag
issues.description: The factory Starlink infotainment system is slow and prone to freezing. Bluetooth connectivity issues are common.

buyingTips: The EJ257's ringland issue is the defining concern — ask about tuning, maintenance history, and any prior engine work
A custom protune from a reputable Subaru tuner is far safer than Cobb OTS maps — ask what tune is loaded
Check for oil consumption — burn 1 quart per 3,000 miles is not unusual but more than that is concerning
The VA STI was the final EJ-powered STI — consider whether you want the character of the EJ or the reliability of the FA engine in the base WRX
Verify timing belt service history — the EJ257 requires belt replacement every 60,000 miles
